entry.title: Re: 🔴 FlashPipe - Your CI/CD Companion for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content: can we use Azure for CICD ?

entry.title: How to create a button to filter table to today date
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want a table that displays sales info that has 3 buttons that will change the date filter of this table, the 3 buttons being:

Button 1 - Yesterday Date 

Button 2 MTD Date 

Button 3 YTD Date 

Can any one tell me how to do this?

entry.title: Details JOB
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I would like ask an help.

I have a custom program that submits a standard program via job and it calls another standard program via job.

Is there a way to find the complete chain?

I would like to explain better:

I execute mi custom program via job, it calls first standard program via job and this one calls another standard program via job. 

How can I retrieve the sequence of all jobs?

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Can you tell me anyone what is the solution of this error and I am facing in fb60 invoice posting
Part 3: Principals and a Framework of Change
entry.content:
Last week, I attended the SAP Transformation Excellence Summit in Chicago. Rouven Morato, General Manager of SAP Signavio, delivered a keynote on the three primary drivers of change: internal, external, and technology. He highlighted the significant pressure AI is exerting on industries to evolve. As I listened, I couldn't help but draw parallels between his points and the topics I've been exploring in my blog. While many people know the ongoing transformation, I aim to provide this community with specific concepts and terminology to foster productive discussions.

The Three Faces of Change: Aging, Evolution, and Revolution

In an earlier blog post, I compared the current OCM landscape to alchemy and said we need to adopt a more structured and systematic approach to change. If we are going to do that, we need to discuss and understand the different ways that change occurs.

Understanding the various forms and drivers of change is crucial for organizations to adapt with agility. Change can be subtle, gradual, or disruptive. Recognizing these different structures allows us to anticipate, adapt, and even lead change effectively. 

Therefore, we created this Group to help you connect with peers and drive these transformations more confidently." Something along those lines might help show the group's value

Aging: The Inevitable Decline

Aging is perhaps the most familiar form of change. It's the gradual deterioration of functions over time. In business, this might manifest as declining sales, market share, or customer satisfaction. A clear example can be seen in professional golf.

The average PGA Tour player's score increases with age. This is a predictable, gradual decline due to factors like physical deterioration and slower reflexes. While some players defy this trend, the overall pattern is clear: aging inevitably affects performance.

Rouven did not address this type of change in his keynote, but we will explore it in this blog post.  Importantly, when people say the only constant is change, they are referring to the effects of aging. Good and proactive Business Transformation Champions need to be aware of this and develop strategies to combat the negative effects of aging while drawing on the strength of new ideas. 

Evolution: Gradual Transformation 

Drawing parallels from Charles Darwin's theory of evolution, we can understand how organizations can evolve over time. Just as the eohippus, a small, four-toed ancestor of the modern horse, gradually evolved into the larger, single-toed horse we know today, organizations can also undergo a series of incremental changes. In biology, these changes occur due to changing environmental factors: deforestation, new predators, scarcity of food, etc.

While any individual creature is nearly indistinguishable from its predecessor, the cumulative changes over vast periods of time become evident. Similarly, organizations may not experience dramatic transformations overnight, but the gradual accumulation of smaller changes can lead to significant shifts in their capabilities and market position.  From Rouven’s keynote, evolution happens due to external factors: changing market conditions, competition, supply chain challenges, etc.

This evolutionary approach to organizational change often requires patience and persistence. It's about building upon existing strengths and making steady progress, rather than attempting radical transformations that may be unsustainable or disruptive. By understanding the principles of biological evolution, organizations can develop a more sustainable and adaptable approach to growth and change. 

Revolution: A Paradigm Shift 

Revolutionary change is a radical departure from the status quo. It's a seismic shift that transforms industries, markets, and even societies. Thomas Kuhn, in his seminal work "The Structure of Scientific Revolutions," described how scientific paradigms shift dramatically in response to anomalies that the old paradigm cannot explain.

In business, revolutionary change can be sparked by disruptive technologies, changing customer preferences, or unforeseen economic conditions. It requires courage, innovation, and a willingness to risk everything.

As a Business Transformation Champion, what is key to note about Revolutionary change is that the driver is internal.  Something is broken about the way the organization us run and needs fixing.  Thomas Kuhn describes these as anomalies that the existing scientific paradigm cannot explain.  Similarly, an organization might look inside itself and see that a process might take too long or might not yield desired outcomes.  That process would need to be fixed.

The negative, but normal reaction to a potential Revolution

Getting back to Kuhn, he describes two behaviors when anomalies are identified.  Adherents to the old paradigm will try to modify what exists to accommodate the new requirement.  This is effectively trying to patch the system.  It’s natural for this to be the default position: don’t throw out the old and replace it, fix what we’ve got.  But if the requirement is internal and there are real problems with the process, then a revolution is required. Insanity is to do things the same way (or close to the same way) and expect different results.  Patching the problem is safe, but ultimately, you’re just kicking the can down the road and delaying the inevitable paradigm shift that’s coming.  Instead, it’s better to be the revolutionary.  Bring new ideas.  Test them against the old requirements and the new ones.  Provide a fresh point of view on the challenges accounting for all the new data and inputs.  This is the crux of what it means to be a Business Transformation Champion. 

Interplay of the Three

It's important to note that these three types of change are not mutually exclusive. They can often coexist and interact in complex ways. For instance, a company might undergo a gradual evolutionary process (product improvement) while simultaneously preparing for a potential revolutionary disruption (new market entry).

Understanding these different structures of change can help leaders make more informed decisions. By recognizing the signs of aging, embracing evolutionary opportunities, and preparing for potential revolutions, organizations can increase their chances of long-term success. 

How do you see these three types of change manifesting in your industry or organization?

entry.title: Re: Query for Prodution order nº, Receipt from Production...
entry.content:
Hi Bruno,

which date?

It is also important that you can filter by date.

 

 Should it be a date range as well?

entry.title: Re: Query for Prodution order nº, Receipt from Production...

Hi Lothar,

Sorry maybe i wasn't clear. what I want is to be able to specify the beginning and end of the research.

Bruno

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87013047 with matnr more than 18 digits.
entry.content:
When you look at transaction definition, you find it executes transaction START_REPORT with parameters RW and 7E2A

  • So it's a Report Writer generated report, Report group is 7E2A, look at it with GR53
  • Then navigate to library and list of reports (display an area menu such as FRGW or search in your menu)
  • 😔old documentation

entry.title: Re: Friends helping friends solve the SAP Crossword puzzle

If you still need anymore information, please reply 

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Broadcasting Stories
entry.content: Hi there is a schedule publication feature, have you managed to sort this?

entry.title: Re: M-series Apple Chip MacBooks and Abap Platform Trial containers using Docker and Podman
entry.content:
Hi @julieplummer20 , Hi @dylan-drummond 

Short update from my site. I've did some short tests with new MacOs Version Sequoia. Thought it would be worth a try, as apple announced especially in the release notes of the game port tool kit that rosetta2 will now support the avs2 instruction set. In contrast, the Rosetta 2 release notes for Sequoia still contain a bug regarding Intel emulation. So it is not quite clear to me what should work and what should not. Anyways.

The old 1909 image is working again natively! with Docker Desktop and Orbstack ( Which is so fast on my MAC M1 ;), huge performance gain). The 2022 Image is still not starting with the known shared memory error during hdbutil startup. I will investigate this a bit in the next week. 

Bests Tom

PS: Also I've found some blog-post where some guys down ported the rosetta 2 version, to the version of MacOS 14.1 with full AVS2 support successfully. Maybe I'will investigate this to.

entry.title: Crystal report to PDF conversion has data formatting issues
entry.content:
Hi,

We recently installaed CRforVS13SP30_0-10010309.EXE in our development environment for the purpose of converting crystal report to PDF using a .NET applciation but the generated PDF has some data formatting issues like data truncations, data alignment, data duplication, etc.,

Can someone help us what could be the problem and suggest a possible solution.

Thanks.

Regards,

AMPrasad

entry.title: Re: Weird FIORI behaviour after system copy
entry.content: I'm not aware of any specific post step for FIORI. What I usually do is running the same activation scripts (STC01) as if it was a new system. These scripts ask for all the necessary parameters in order to run the system.

entry.title: Re: selecting a cell in sac table

and Solved: SAC APP Designer - How to display selected value i... - SAP Community

Best regards

entry.title: Employee Time Calculations
entry.content:
Hi @S_Strangfeld 

We have been trying to default start time and endtime in leave type for half day. After unclick fullday check box of leave type  or selecting of half day leave type system is not defaulting timings. I have attached the relevant snapshots , please help on this problem.

I have refered your post  Good rules - good business - New rule functions fo... - SAP Community

Business Rules Created to default End Time.

  1. MDF_ET_Default_StartEnd_Time

 

2. In MDF Object “ Employee Time”   for  fields Time type and start Date field  Business Rule “MDF_ET_Default_StartEnd_Time” is configured.

3. Unclick the Full day check of half day

4. End time is not defaulting with Business Rules conditions.

 

Time Type Configurations

if you are looking for API's for change purchase order in Sourcing & Procurement. pls refer below link for standard API's

APIs in Sourcing and Procurement | SAP Help Portal

 or are you looking for the purchase order changes, like we see in table CDHDR & CDPOS?     

Thanks

Sathish

entry.title: Re: Weird FIORI behaviour after system copy
entry.content: I assume TST means TEST and I have no clue what you mean by INT system. However It's hard to tell without knowing how this tile was created. For instance, the wrong way of defining a tile/target mapping would be using the complete URL pointing to a app (including host + port). I recommend start checking in RZ10/RZ11 or even in SICF (display host and port information) to check if somehow the host of both system matches. If that is the case the system profile must be adjusted.

entry.title: Re: Weird FIORI behaviour after system copy
entry.content: I assume TST means TEST and I have no clue what you mean by INT system. However It's hard to tell without knowing how this tile was created. For instance, the wrong way of defining a tile/target mapping would be using the complete URL pointing to a app (including host + port). I recommend start checking in RZ10/RZ11 or even in SICF (display host and port information) to check if somehow the host of both system matches. If that is the case the system profile must be adjusted.

entry.title: Re: SAP Private Link service use cases for SAP Cloud Integration and SAP Build Work Zone, Standard E
entry.content:
Hi Gowrisankar,

Managed App Router support for SAP Private Link for is quite important for us, we have lots of NEO Portals with hundreds of apps that need to be migrated to CF. 

Having to rewire every app with a approuter would be a lot of work (also we dont like the App Developers having a say where the connection should go, we prefer the managed option by far).

Is there any news when and if this will be supported ?

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Weird FIORI behaviour after system copy
entry.content: HI Fabio, Yes TSTS means TEST. INT means Quality env. So, i does not understand how i can targert to Quality while i did a refresh from PROD.

entry.title: Re: selecting a cell in sac table
entry.content:
Hi Ivan.

What I'm trying to achieve is to filter a detailed table based in which dimension was clicked on a header table. When you click in a country column in the header table, for example, then you filter the detailed table by country. If instead you click in the cost center dimension, then you filter by cost center, and so on.

getSelections returns the whole row, so I cannot know to which dimension corresponds the cell clicked. I can do some workaround like, for example, put a radio button control to control which can of filtering I going to do before clicking in the header table, but it's a little dirty, as requires to click on the radio button each time you want to change the filtering dimension. The ideal solution requires to know to which column belongs the clicked cell, but I cannot found a way to know this when several dimensions are present in the table.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: WCM Control Key
entry.content:
Greetings,

regarding this you have two options:

  • define a default WCM control key to main work center on the technical object this default control key then triggers WCM process
  • define an approval which triggers WCM process, assign this permit to the technical object and inherit it to the plant maintenance order. Make sure on the technial object that you can inherit this approval to maintenance order

The first option just automates defining the WCM control key on the maintenance order. For the second option you need to check the following configuration:

  • SPRO -> Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Work Clearance Management -> Approvals -> Standard -> Maintain Approvals
  • SPRO -> Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Work Clearance Management -> Approvals -> Enhancements -> Assign Approval
  • SPRO -> Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Work Clearance Management -> Approvals -> Activate Work Clearance Management for Maintenance Orders

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: Automatically populate Person Responsible in IW32/33...
entry.content:
Ok, this is an order partner, partners can be many entities: customers, suppliers, employees, etc.

  • So I guess you've found another employee field, but irrelevant as it's not at the correct level
  • Browse the IHPA table “Maintenance: Partners” for an existing order to find the partner function (beware, there's a conversion exit on this field)
  • or (better) run BAPI_ALM_ORDER_GET_DETAIL on this PM order.

entry.title: Re: How to see long text of the error message for stuck q...
entry.content:
Hi @DominikTylczyn,

Thank you for responding to the query, I tried double clicking the error message but nothing happens after clicking.

entry.title: MPN need to flow along with material number and MPN other requirement
entry.content:
Manufacture Part Number Requirement

Dear experts, 

Would appreciate if you can kindly help us with the following MPN requirement of my client.

Requirement of Client:

  • Client requires the MPN number in the material master. The same MPN should be reflected in all places where the Material number flows. For example, MPN should appear in BOM, stock reports, inventory reports etc.)

  • Make (manufacturer) field is required in material master and the same should flow in procurement process (for e.g.: PR, PO, GR, Service Entry sheets and invoices)
  • The explanation of client requirement is given below in 2 cases apart from the SAP best practice

SAP Best Practice

Case 1:

As per SAP best practice, manufacturer as a supplier which is like 18300080(Vendor Number)

 For e.g.: Croma, reliance digital, Amazon

Client requires the MPN numbers based on manufacturer (supplier)

For example, As per SAP:

SAP standard:

Mfr Part Number: Phone12345. (manufacturers or suppliers are, Croma, Reliance digital, Amazon etc..)

Client Requirement:

Mfr Part Number should be Phone 12345 as follows:

  1. In place of manufacturer, the supplier or vendor name should be replaced with manufacturer(Original Equipment Manufacturer-OEM) like Samsung, Nokia and Redmi etc.

SAP standard:

 

MPN

Phone12345

SUPPLIER

Manufacturer

1.CROMA

 

 

2.Reliance Digital

 

 

3.Amazon

 

 

4.Flipkart

 

 

 

 No provision in standard sap best practice

 

OEM

MAKER

1.Samsung

 

 

2.Nokia

 

 

3.Redmi

Client Requirement:

Case 1: As per client team, for supplier they are asking the additional Field as Make (Distributors)

Case 1: Normal scenario

 

 

MPN

Phone 12345

Manufacturer

1.Samsung

 

2.Nokia

 

3.Redmi

Distributor/supplier

1.CROMA

 

2.Reliance Digital

 

3.Amazon

 

4.Flipkart

Case 2 Rare case: MPN will be different, but supplier remains same, For e.g.: phone12345 – CROMA, Reliance, Amazon

Rare case

 

 

 Different MPN

MPN

 

MPN

Manufacturer

1.Samsung

Sphone12345

 

2.Nokia

NPHONE22243

 

3.Redmi

RPhone1624716

Distributor/supplier

1.CROMA

 

 

2.Reliance Digital

 

 

3.Amazon

 

 

4.Flipkart

entry.title: Re: Announcing SAP Inside Track Pune - 19th October 2024

Thanks,

Punith

entry.title: Re: The Fiori tile has an error.
entry.content:
The message in picture 2 seems to be a custom message class ZTNZ001, message number 002.

I understood you got the error in RSRT so the issue would not be in Fiori but in the query.

Is this query created using the custom analytical queries in Fiori? If that is the case if you be fixed there. If not, it should be fixed where this query was created.

I faced similar issues in the past even when some queries were correct by using the "Repair InfoObjects" function in tcode RSD1.

2. Lectures and alignment
3. Volunteers and their distribution
4.  Marketing and Youtube Broadcasting

The bests regards

entry.title: Data conversion Plant Maintenance
entry.content:
Is there any recommended conversion strategy to move rom standard SAP PM to new S/4 Phase model. The main focus my question is on the transactional data conversion (Notification, Maintenance Orders). Not talking about creating new orders , but how to transfer orders that are released and being processed , already have Purchase Requisitions and Purchase orders assigned , Materias reserved and partially delivered, services requested and partially confirmed aso. 

Thanks for your feedback in advance.

Can you please help me how to locate error message in the XML screen?

entry.title: Change outbound crossNavigation in Fiori adaption project
entry.content:
I've created an adaption project of a Fiori Elements app. There are outbound crossNavigation definition in the manifest.json. I want to change one of the outbound navigations to a custom app instead of the standard app. Is it possible to redefine this somehow? Haven't found anything the help documentation. 

Thanks and regards

entry.title: Re: Organization Team SAP Inside Track Porto 2024
entry.content: The event took place in a restaurant in Vila Nova de Gaia, where members of the organization and volunteers participated. The open issues were resolved, the toasts were presented, and the dinner was excellent!

entry.title: Re: API Masking/ API Routing Via SAP API Management: A Step by Step Guide
entry.content:
Thank you for your comment!

Yes, you can use an API Provider pointing to S/4HANA (e.g., API_SALES_ORDER_SRV) instead of a URL. The HTTP 401 error usually relates to an authorization issue. Please check the following:

 

1. Authentication: Ensure you're using the correct method (OAuth2, Basic Auth, etc.).

2. Cloud Connector: Verify the destination setup and credentials.

3. User Roles: Ensure the user has access to API_SALES_ORDER_SRV.

4. CSRF Token: For certain operations, a CSRF token might be needed.

Addressing these should give a good idea to resolve the issue. Let me know if the issue got resolved or not.

entry.title: Re: POSC Config for Non-HU Product
entry.content:
hi Juergen, i tried to perform the outbound again. Both OBD i use same product and same bin (1 product with HU and another NON-HU), the WORC and storage process are trigger for both OBD...but it doesnt trigger POSC for the OBD pick from NON-HU

Current set up LOSC + POSC

S.type > intermediate bin > pack center > GI

Issue: When picking from HU product: s.type > intermediate bin > GI 

So we need to enter LIST in RF to change destination everytime...

entry.title: Re: Query for Prodution order nº, Receipt from Production...
entry.content: Production order.

entry.title: Format not compatible with SAP Business One 1000130; contact format provider for support
entry.content:
Dear all,

While executing Financial Reports which is provided by SAP then it is showing error like "Format not compatible with SAP Business One 1000130; contact format provider for support"

SAP Business One

Regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: Re: Error in application when deploying it in workzone
entry.content: I faced similar issues when extending some HCM apps and if was way easier to deploy the app in the on premise system instead of BTP. Unfortunately not everything which can be done in ui5.yaml files can be done in xs-app.json (or at least not in the same way). Also, in workzone is pretty simple to create an app pointing to an SAPUi5 app in an on-premise system - you only need the destination + app id. Moreover, when deploying apps to BTP it's always good to add the "sap.platform.cf" : {"ui5VersionNumber": "1.120.x" } in the manifest file. This is the SAPUI5 version used by workzone when opening an app there. If you do not specify it, work zone always use the most recent SAPUI5 version from SAP CDN. This might break the app or at least will make it slower as in this scenario the library won't be cached.

entry.title: Error "Enter a value for field Name 1 of organization" using the SAP_COM_0008
entry.content:
Using the Communication scenario SAP_COM_0008 - BP_SUITEBULK_REPL_REQ_IN web service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, the error: "Enter a value for field Name 1 of organization." appears in the payload, Can you confirm which field is required here?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Weird FIORI behaviour after system copy
entry.content: I found the issue. It was 2 HTTP RFC targeting the bad system.

entry.title: Re: Error "Enter a value for field Name 1 of organization...
entry.content:
The data that is required to be filled is as follows:

<Organisation>
     <Name>
      <FormOfAddressCode xsi:nil="true" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/>
      <FirstLineName xsi:nil="true" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/> <<<< this is the field that is required within the payload
      <SecondLineName xsi:nil="true" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/>
      <ThirdLineName xsi:nil="true" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/>
      <FourthLineName xsi:nil="true" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"/>

Please review the following:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/f4edce62151b421bbb45d4c92a98b9fc.html?locale=en-USBusiness Partner - Replicate from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Clien

entry.title: Re: Print preview function is greyed out in Change Journa...
entry.content:
Hi Kazuhiro

Thanks for providing the product version, now I could simulate the same issue as you reported.

Checking into the same function in SAP GUI the same phenomenon exists there. So my conclusion is there may be exist the option listed there in the menu as this is belong to the standard normal lists but only work for some scenarios while it does not work for your current transaction.

Please take the attached file as a reference. As no any official documentation about this, what I can do is just check this in the OP system to get the comparation for you.

 

Kind Regards

Lucy

entry.title: Re: Different appearance of UI elements in Belize/Quartz on R3/S4
entry.content:
Found it.

Deactivate SAP fiori features.

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/sap_gui_for_windows/63bd20104af84112973ad59590645513/5d8d19d6680e4b25a8aa879c09de8f62.html?version=8.00&locale=us-US

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano,

My name is Nguyen Thi Ngoc Ha, and I am currently a third-year student in the Faculty of Information Systems at the University of Economics and Law (UEL). I am from Vietnam and am still pursuing my studies, but I have a strong passion for ERP solutions within the SAP system.

As a Vietnamese student and a first-generation college attendee in my family, I aspire to contribute to the development of the economic field both domestically and internationally.

Becoming a professional SAP consultant is my goal for the future, and I believe that obtaining an SAP certification will help me enhance my skills and add greater value to the companies I will work for. I kindly ask you to consider my application for the SAP certification program.

Sincerely,
Nguyen Thi Ngoc Ha

entry.title: Re: How can an independent consultant purchase a new 'red...
entry.content:
Hi Marcus,

As you seem to be seeing a different view I feel I can't advise you correctly without being able to also view.

Can I please ask you to send an email into sales@sap.com, we can then set up a screenshare call to determine if we need assistance from IT.

Thanks

Sheena

entry.title: Re: Unable to do SMT Mapping for the fields present in MARC_APS_EXT Table.
entry.content: Hi mines, what did you end up deciding on this in the end? We're facing exactly the same challenge. For some reason none of the Advanced Planning Fields from MARC_APS_EXT are not available in standard MDG Data Model or UI but we need to maintain them via MDG. Custom Entity or you found an alternative solution?

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Speed up your SAP Cloud Integration Development with CPI-Helper
entry.content: CPI-Helper already in use. It's a great help and I love it 🙂 Thank you Dominik

entry.title: Re: How to setup low value assets
entry.content:
Well, business process for LVA is no different from the other asset classes, that is the reason you don't find it separately in J62. The configuration specific to LVA is 'Specify Amounts (SSCUI ID 102610). SAP Standard provides LVA asset class 5000, of course customers can define their own along with other configuration pertaining to AA in general. 

Thank You!

entry.title: Re: Password change in ECTR
Re: Automatically populate Person Responsible in IW32/33...
entry.content: Okay, got it. In this case the person number PERS_NO field corresponds to person responsible and through debug mode I could find that the value is getting populated by reading infotypes. Now this concept seems totally alien to me. Moreover I am not able to find out which table this field value is stored in. This is standard sap code and field, so can you help me out pls?

entry.title: Re: Password change in ECTR
entry.content:
Hello Gian Carlo,

this is already addressed with a customer ticket and our support organisation is working on this currently. Please stay tuned.

Regards

Gerhard Himmelsbach - Product Manager SAP ECTR

entry.title: Re: Vendor notes and Attachments data migration from ECC...
entry.content:
Hi Ahalya,

Pls check with the communication scenario SAP_COM_0008 there is an inbound service for attachments. pls check if its helpful.

Attachments | SAP Help Portal

Thanks

Sathish

entry.title: Re: Query for Prodution order nº, Receipt from Production...
entry.content:
Try this

/*Gets SI/SO according to Production Order*/
/*Filter by Date/DocNum*/
/*Filter can be empty*/

/**SELECT FROM [OWOR] T0 **/
DECLARE @StartDate AS DATE
/* WHERE */
SET @StartDate = /* T0.PostDate */ '[%0]'

/**SELECT FROM [OWOR] T1 **/
DECLARE @EndDate AS Date
/* WHERE */
SET @EndDate = /* T1.PostDate */ '[%1]'

/**SELECT FROM [OWOR] T2 **/
DECLARE @DocNum AS INT
/* WHERE */
SET @DocNum = /* T2.DocNum */ '[%2]'

/*DEBUG ONLY*/
--DECLARE @StartDate AS DATE
--SET @StartDate = '20240901'

--DECLARE @EndDate AS  DATE
--SET @EndDate = '20240924'

--DECLARE @DocNum AS INT
--SET @DocNum = ''--27894

SELECT
DISTINCT
	 [OWOR].[PostDate]
	,[OWOR].[DocNum] AS [Prodution order nº]
	,[OIGN].[DocNum] AS [Receipt from Production (SI)]
	,[OIGE].[DocNum] AS [Issue for Production(SO)]
FROM
	[OWOR]
	LEFT JOIN [IGE1] ON [IGE1].[BaseEntry] = [OWOR].[DocEntry] AND [IGE1].[BaseType] = [OWOR].[ObjType]
	LEFT JOIN [OIGE] ON [OIGE].[DocEntry] = [IGE1].[DocEntry] 
	LEFT JOIN [IGN1] ON [IGN1].[BaseEntry] = [OWOR].[DocEntry] AND [IGN1].[BaseType] = [OWOR].[ObjType]
	LEFT JOIN [OIGN] ON [OIGN].[DocEntry] = [IGN1].[DocEntry] 
WHERE 
	([OWOR].[PostDate] >= @StartDate OR @StartDate = '')
	AND 
	(OWOR.PostDate <= @EndDate OR @EndDate = '')
	AND
	([OWOR].[DocNum] = @DocNum OR @DocNum = '')

ORDER BY 
	[OWOR].[PostDate] ASC, [OWOR].[DocNum]

FOR BROWSE

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Setup of Synthetic User Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Great,

Thanks a lot ! so useful.

entry.title: Re: Weird FIORI behaviour after system copy

entry.title: Re: Time-Based Filtering in SAC Stories: Using Model Variables for Measure Restriction
entry.content:
Thank you Sebastian for sharing this.

Does your Restricted measure shows figures at aggregated levels of Date dimension (Quarter, Year, and All) ?

Br, Oleksandr

entry.title: Re: Details JOB
entry.content:
Without more information, answer is 'no'

  • If you are lucky, you may find a message, in the log of the first background job, related to creation of the second one.
  • Else analyze the standard report 
  • Useful FM : BP_JOB_READ, BP_JOBLOG_READ
  • You could look for potentially related jobs by analyzing the creation date of job 2 within the execution period of job 1, but this won't be absolute proof.

entry.title: Re: CDS --profile hybrid
entry.content: cds serve --profile hybrid --resolve-bindings

does anybody knows which permissions necessary für connection SAP user?

BR

Gerhard

entry.title: Re: Error connecting to Crystal Runtime; check the instal...

Hi,

The problem was solved by reinstalling SAP to have the crystal runtime compatible with my SAP;
that's it.

regards.

entry.title: Single Sign On with SHA-256 not working
entry.content:
Hi - we use SHA-1 for SSO via ADFS-Services and are forced by security-issues to use SHA-256.

But when we change on the Windows ADFS-Server from SHA1 to SHA256 login to SAP Cloud4Customer does not work!

Any idea?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Create Selenium scripts for SAP Cloud ALM Synthetic User Monitoring
entry.content:
Thank you so much for such a nice blog.

just one question as I didn't understand correctly. for example if we have 100 users and want to monitor their actions in web applications, we need to install the Selenium IDE extension in each user's browsers? and we need to have different scripts for different web applications?

BR,

Farnaz

entry.title: How to Properly Refresh a Page on Side Drawer Navigation in SAP MDK
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working on a Mobile Development Kit (MDK) project, and I need some guidance on refreshing a page when navigating via the side drawer.

Here's the scenario: I want the target page to refresh automatically when a user selects a page from the side drawer. I tried adding a refresh action in the OnSuccess event of the NavigationAction. However, this seems to refresh the previous page (the one before navigation), not the target page.

The strange part is that this same approach works for other navigations (like selecting an entry in an Object Table), but it doesn’t seem to work for the side drawer navigation. Has anyone faced this issue or found a solution for refreshing the target page on side drawer navigation?

Any suggestions or alternatives to get this working would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

Here’s the code I’m using to refresh the page:

 

export default function Refresh_page(clientAPI) {
  var page = clientAPI.getPageProxy();
  var mypage = '#Page:' + page._page.id
  alert(mypage)
  clientAPI.evaluateTargetPath(mypage).redraw();
  
  return;
}

entry.title: Re: Tasks not displaying in process quality tasks app and manage tasks app
entry.content:
Hello RaghavanKathamuthu,

in general, Quality Tasks and Quality Notification Tasks are two different things, please see the documentation:
Task | SAP Help Portal

Create Quality Tasks | SAP Help Portal

Best regards

Jonathan

entry.title: Crystal Reports for Visual Studio - Developer and Runtime
entry.content:
Hi,

As we are new using this CRforVS13SP30_0-10010309.EXE  (Crystal Reports, developer version for Microsoft Visual Studio) for our requirement of generating a PDF for a crystal report using this package. But while deploying to server with this package installation, it's asking for Visual studio has to installed.

But we found a ticket Ref where it's mentioned as

" The developer edition (.exe download) provides the integration with Visual Studio. The various runtime installs are for use when deploying your application after it is developed."

and also suggested as

"1. The development and runtime installs should have the same Service Pack, so it's usually a smart choice to download both at the same time."

But we are not find a place where we can upgrade CRforVS13SP30_0-10010309.EXE as well as to download a runtime of this upgrade version.

Please help us on this.

Thanks.

Regards,

Maruthi Prasad A

entry.title: Re: Automatically populate Person Responsible in IW32/33...
entry.content:
Field would be IHPA-PARNR

  • Look at FM such as PM_GET_PARTNER_INFO or FM of group IPAR ,such as PM_PARTNER_READ or PM_PARTNER_READ_MASTER_DATA
  • Some documentation is available online
  • Some blog on PM tables

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Hi @Ashok_R 

This add-in really is a great feature!!!
The sad thing about it is that stories in ODE with scripting are not supported. Scripting is such a great feature in ODE and I use it a lot. 
When will the add-in support stories with scripting?

Best regards,
DWE

entry.title: Customized catalogs desapeared after upgrade
entry.content:
Hello,

Something strange happened after FIORI Upgrade to release SAP FIORI FOR S4HANA 2023 01 (02/2024) 

all my custom catalogs desapeared, then I have imported custom catalogs from another system, but again, after client copy is has desapeared.

I don't know what is the trigger that causes this. today, the custom catalogs were actualy in the content manager but not in the designer anymore.

Any idea? any report to synchronize ? why does that desappear?

 

Thank you !!

entry.title: Re: CDS --profile hybrid
entry.content:
Thank you for pointing it out  @Dinu 

To resolve the issue when using cds run --profile hybrid or cds serve --profile hybrid connecting to the HANA Database instead of local SQLite, try adding the --resolve-bindings flag.

This ensures that the credentials of the service bindings from the cloud are correctly pulled

cds serve --profile hybrid --resolve-bindings

For example, when running your app in the development profile with cds:

cds serve --profile development --resolve-bindings

This will make sure the application retrieves service bindings (e.g., credentials for external databases like HANA) from the cloud even while using the development profile.

This setup helps simulate cloud services locally during development, ensuring consistency when switching to hybrid or production environments.

Feature added on:

entry.title: AR INVOICE ITEM ROW TOTALFC
entry.content:
Hi All

There is no INV1.LineTotalFC though there is INV1.LineTotal?  Is it the only way is to manually calculate by INV1.Price (After Discount) x T1. Quantity to get LineTotal in Foreign BP Currency?

There are both OINV.DocTotal and OINV.DocTotalFC.

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Re: Production Order Settlement Error
entry.content:
Hi Olivia,

I think the error described by Dilay is just for orders that have periodic settlement. Normally, the workaround using posting period 8 and settlement period 7 is needed only for periodic settled orders.

Do you agree?

Thanks.

BR,

Filipe

entry.title: Re: Auto populate standard text in ME21N and manage purch...
entry.content:
Hi,

Generally standard Texts (SO01) are used to populates the texts in the output of transactional documents.

maybe refer the useful blog below shows how the standard text can be used in S/4HANA cloud.

S/4HANA Cloud - Managing and Integrating Standard ... - SAP Community

Thanks

Sathish

entry.title: CDS VIew Multiple Row for Status
entry.content:
Hello i am developing a CDS view which should extract some information from EWM Outbound table "/scdl/db_proci_o": 

There are status fields like picking, packing, good issue. 
problem is if i query this i get for each outbound document multiple status values but i just want to get the current one.
Also was is strange is that the results seam not correct as i have every time the same pattern "N,N,Y" which makes no sense as the good issue cannot be completed when picking is not completed. 

If i enable the last change field i clearly see that the last change has all on "Y" = 9 (completed) and i just want to fetch the latest changed data. 

I know there are table functions (row number , rank, sorting etc), but i am still not sure if this is the right track. 




CDS example status: 

case

when ewmOutbound.status_pick = '9' then 'Y'

when ewmOutbound.status_pick != '9' then 'N'

else ewmOutbound.status_pick

end as Picking,

case

when ewmOutbound.status_pick = '9' and ewmOutbound.status_gi = '9' then 'Y'

else 'N'

end as Packing,

case

when ewmOutbound.status_gi = '9' then 'Y'

when ewmOutbound.status_gi != '9' then 'N'

else ewmOutbound.status_gi

end as Good_Issue,

In a table function i make ROW NUMBER() and assign them a row number.
ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY TO_TIMESTAMP(ZSACCONTROLCON.latestchanged, 'YYYYMMDDHH24MISS') DESC) AS DateRank,

i though i just make DateRank = 1 in a where condition after the table function but now i saw the results and i am wondering why there is no number 1. 

I highlighted the latest change as you see row 3, but i dont know how to only extract always the latestchange. 

 

 

 

 

Many thanks for any support!

entry.content:

SAP Business One 

Regards,

Nikunj Mehta

entry.title: Re: POSC Config for Non-HU Product
entry.content:
"

hi Juergen, i tried to perform the outbound again. Both OBD i use same product and same bin (1 product with HU and another NON-HU), the WORC and storage process are trigger for both OBD...but it doesnt trigger POSC for the OBD pick from NON-HU

Current set up LOSC + POSC

S.type > intermediate bin > pack center > GI

Issue: When picking from HU product: s.type > intermediate bin > GI 

So we need to enter LIST in RF to change destination everytime... "

But according to your screenshot the system does use the process. 

How do you determine the pack work center?

Brgds

Juergen

entry.title: Get all tables mapped to an OBJECTCLASS in CDHDR
entry.content:
Dear all

I like to know a way to get all SAP ERP tables that get updated when an entry for a particular OBJECTCLASS is logged in CDHDR table. I see that there is an entry logged in CDHDR with OBJECTCLASS called "BELEG". I understood that this could mean several tables like BSEG, BKPF, BSID, BSAD have got documents updated within them.

I noticed that updates (Us) in SAP ERP are not getting logged in CDPOS table. So, I like to rely on CDHDR and get list of all tables that got updated for a particular UDATE & OBJECTCLASS. Using this, I will check if data is fresh in my target system (Databricks).

appreciate ideas/thoughts.

Br,

Noor.

entry.title: Packing Issue due to missed stock posting in Inspection lot and QMHU table
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our client is setup HU managed warehouse (not EWM). Inspection lots are getting created at HU level. We are facing a weird issue on random basis.

At the time of Goods Receipt from external suppliers, Inspection lot gets generated at HU level and postings (321/311) happened on UD. QMHU table also updated properly.

But while doing goods receival process for STO, Inspection lot gets generated at HU level, but postings (321/311) does not happen on UD completion. And QMHU table is updating without stock posting (field: QMGBUCHHUM). When we try to use the same (batch) stock for goods issue with packing, system does not allow to complete packing and throws a dump  ( DBSQL_DUPLICATE_KEY_ERROR ) where system tries to insert the duplicate entry into QMHU for specific inspection lot and new packing handling unit.

To sort this issue temporarily, we try to debug the dump and try to remove the duplicates from QMHU. which further allows to pack for goods issue.

If anyone has experienced the same issue and have the detailed information about root cause and resolution. Please share the same.

Thank you in advance !

entry.title: CCM - Settlement date after contract end date
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

Is it possible to add manual settlement date after contract end date? 

Example.  Contract Period 01.01.2024 to 15.03.2024, Final settlement done on 15.03.2024 but still want to another settlement on 20.04.2024

Currently, I am getting an error. Error msg no WB2R E 029 & WB2R E 199.

Any solution for this issue?

entry.content:
Hello,

I'm getting above subjected error while posting Item Interest Calculation.

I have uploaded the TCODE FINT Calculation screen in attachment.

There is no payment Term 001 available in DB still why I'm getting this error as payment term 001 not defined. I have configured 0001 standard Payment term which is correctly configured.

Please advise how to resolve this error?

 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Reassign costs and revenues - new app without excel upload???
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have just read that there is a new version of the ‘Reassign costs and revenues’ app (F2009 --> F2009A) and wanted to try it out straight away. I noticed that the ‘Upload Excel’ feature, which is very important for my customer, is not included in the new app, while the previous app is now marked as ‘old’ and will probably disappear soon. That can't be true, can it? Will this be added? What is the alternative way to reassign via Excel?

Thank you in advance for any hint.

Best regards

Rado

entry.title: Re: Created accrual item types cannot be selected in accr...

For me official documentation that you mentioned is not clear, for example they mentioned about BAdI: Additional Account Assignments that is not exist in the PUBLIC. Do you have maybe an official SAP note that confirm that this is standard behaviour of the system?

entry.title: Choosing the Right Path for Your S/4HANA Transformation: Greenfield, Brownfield, or Bluefield?
entry.content:
The journey to SAP S/4HANA can take different forms, depending on your organization's goals, existing system landscape, and appetite for change. Whether you're looking to redesign your processes entirely, preserve your current system, or take a more flexible, hybrid approach, selecting the right migration strategy is critical.

In this blog, we'll explore the key differences between greenfield, brownfield, and bluefield conversions, helping you determine which path aligns best with your business needs as you transition to S/4HANA.

Greenfield Approach:

A greenfield approach involves starting from scratch. It’s a fresh implementation of S/4HANA, where the system is built without carrying over any existing customizations, processes, or data. Companies use greenfield when they want to redesign their business processes entirely or when they are moving from a non-SAP system to S/4HANA.

Brownfield Approach:

A brownfield approach involves a system conversion, upgrading the existing SAP ERP (ECC) system to S/4HANA. It retains most of the existing customizations, processes, and data, but moves them onto the S/4HANA platform.

Companies opt for brownfield when they want to retain existing processes and customizations but upgrade to S/4HANA.

Features:

  • It's an in-place migration, where you upgrade the existing system rather than rebuilding or starting fresh.
  • Keeps historical data, configurations, and custom developments in place.
  • Suitable for companies looking to maintain continuity and minimize disruptions.
  • Less costly and faster than a Greenfield approach but may carry over technical debt or outdated processes.

Use Case: When an organization wants to preserve its existing investments and customizations while upgrading the core to S/4HANA.

Bluefield Approach:

The bluefield approach is a hybrid of greenfield and brownfield. It involves selective data and process migration, allowing companies to retain some legacy processes and data while adopting new ones.

When companies want to modernize certain parts of their processes but also preserve critical customizations and data from the old system.

Features:

  • It allows for selective data migration, enabling you to bring over relevant historical data and customizations without carrying over everything.
  • Offers the flexibility to redesign and optimize parts of the system while retaining valuable aspects from the legacy system.
  • Reduces downtime compared to Greenfield and allows better customization compared to Brownfield.
  • Often involves the use of specialized tools to filter out unwanted data and processes, enabling selective transformation.

Use Case: Ideal for organizations that want to take advantage of S/4HANA's new features while selectively transferring necessary custom developments, processes, and data.

Other Approaches

Selective Data Transition: This is sometimes considered a variant of bluefield, where specific data and configurations are migrated rather than doing a full system conversion or a full reimplementation.

Phased or Iterative Migrations: In these cases, parts of the system or specific modules are converted incrementally to S/4HANA, rather than performing a full conversion all at once.

Summary of differences:

These three approaches (greenfield, brownfield, bluefield) cover the main strategies for S/4HANA migrations, and each organization chooses based on its specific needs, resources, and goals.

So what to choose? My experience and industry recommendation is Bluefield, which offers more flexibility and the ability to modernize selectively, whereas Brownfield is faster but less transformative.

Q) SAP is speaking a lot about Clean Core. Which one is more relevant in this context?

As per my knowledge, the Bluefield approach is more aligned with the Clean Core concept in SAP S/4HANA.

Here’s why:

  • Clean Core Concept: The idea of a clean core is to minimize or completely avoid customizations in the core SAP system, ensuring that the core remains as standard as possible. Any required customizations should be done using side-by-side extensions or through the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), rather than modifying the core directly. This enables easier future upgrades, better system stability, and enhanced use of cloud innovations.
  • Bluefield’s Relevance:
  • In a Bluefield migration, you have the ability to selectively migrate custom developments, business processes, and data. This allows you to critically evaluate which customizations are truly necessary and eliminate unnecessary custom code that would otherwise clutter the system.
  • It supports modernization of your landscape by allowing you to adopt new, standard SAP best practices for certain areas, while keeping only the required custom elements in external environments or using modern extensibility frameworks.You can move toward a clean core by offloading custom code into more flexible extension options, ensuring the core SAP S/4HANA system remains standard and optimized for upgrades.

On the other hand, Brownfield typically carries over all existing configurations and customizations, which is counterproductive to maintaining a clean core, as it could perpetuate legacy customizations and technical debt.

Thus, Bluefield is the better choice if your goal is to implement a Clean Core strategy.

Q) What additional points I must consider with regards to Security & Authorization in SAP?

When considering Security & Authorization in an SAP S/4HANA migration or conversion project, whether it's Bluefield or Brownfield, there are critical factors you must address to ensure the system remains secure and compliant post-migration.

1. Role Design and Optimization:

  • Review Existing Roles: In a Brownfield migration, all legacy roles and authorizations are typically carried over. This can result in outdated or redundant roles that do not align with S/4HANA's simplified processes. For a Clean Core strategy, assess and clean up roles to ensure they match new processes and business needs.
    • Simplified Data Model: S/4HANA has a simplified data model, which may affect authorization objects linked to tables, transactions, and business processes. You will need to map legacy roles to the new S/4HANA authorization objects.
    • Optimize for Fiori: S/4HANA heavily uses SAP Fiori as the front-end, which comes with a role-based access control (RBAC) model. Ensure that roles are optimized for Fiori apps, as Fiori apps typically involve multiple underlying backend authorizations.

2. Clean Core and Extensibility:

  • Avoid Custom Code in the Core: With the Clean Core concept, custom developments should be moved to external platforms (e.g., SAP Business Technology Platform). Ensure that custom authorization objects related to those developments are also managed outside of the core, where applicable.
  • Extension Model: If your migration uses SAP’s new side-by-side extensibility framework or enhancements via BTP, you must ensure that proper security mechanisms are in place for these external apps, including secure communication, OAuth, and SAML for authentication.

3. Segregation of Duties (SoD) and GRC Alignment:

  • Review SoD Conflicts: S/4HANA introduces new functionalities and removes obsolete ones, so it's essential to review and update your SoD rulesets to account for these changes. Ensure that your SAP GRC Access Control is aligned with the new S/4HANA environment.
  • GRC Integration: Make sure that GRC Access Control, Process Control, and Risk Management are integrated into the new environment to continuously monitor authorizations, SoD, and other risk factors.

NOTE: With the new SAP GRC embedded model, all the GRC components such as AC, PC, and RM will become embedded on S4 HANA. You may also consider evaluating it, if your S4 HANA migration doesn’t have it in the list.

4. New Security Features in S/4HANA:

  • S/4HANA Security Innovations: Leverage S/4HANA’s enhanced security features like Context-Based Authorizations (CBA), Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), and UI Masking or UI Logging to protect sensitive data.
  • Cloud and Hybrid Security: If you are moving to S/4HANA Cloud or adopting a hybrid model, ensure you follow SAP's best practices for cloud security (including SAP Cloud Identity Services) and managing secure access to both on-premise and cloud systems.

5. Compliance and Data Privacy:

  • Data Protection Compliance: Ensure that the authorization design complies with GDPR, CCPA, or any other applicable data privacy regulations. This includes defining clear roles for access to personal or sensitive data and implementing data masking or encryption mechanisms where necessary.
  • Audit and Logging: S/4HANA offers enhanced capabilities for audit logging and monitoring. Ensure that proper logging of sensitive transactions and authorization changes is implemented to facilitate compliance audits.

6. SAML, OAuth, and Single Sign-On (SSO):

  • Enable SSO: Ensure that Single Sign-On (SSO) solutions, such as SAML 3.0 or OAuth 2.0, are correctly set up for secure and seamless access across different systems. This is particularly important in environments that use both cloud and on-premise systems.
  • SAP Identity Authentication and Provisioning: Consider using SAP Cloud Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning Services for secure and centralized user management across your SAP and non-SAP applications.

7. User Experience and Authorization Consistency:

  • Consistent User Experience: Since S/4HANA relies heavily on Fiori applications, ensure that users have consistent authorization experiences across different devices (mobile, desktop) and application layers (back-end, Fiori, etc.).
  • Catalog and Group Management: In Fiori, roles are managed using catalogs and groups (spaces and pages). You should make sure the Fiori launchpad roles are properly configured to give users access only to the apps they need.

8. Testing and Validation:

  • Security Testing: Post-migration, conduct rigorous security testing, including penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and role-based access reviews, to ensure that all aspects of the new system, including extensions and external apps, are secure.
  • Authorization Testing: Run authorization testing to ensure that users have only the access they need and that there are no excessive privileges carried over from legacy systems.

By addressing these security and authorization concerns, you can ensure that your SAP S/4HANA migration is secure, compliant, and optimized for modern business processes.

Q) What is context based authorization?

Context-Based Authorization (CBA) in SAP refers to a dynamic approach to controlling access based on the context in which a user is attempting to perform an action. Unlike traditional role-based authorization, which grants or restricts access solely based on predefined roles and permissions, CBA takes into account additional contextual factors at the time of the authorization check.

Key Elements of Context-Based Authorization:

1. Dynamic Contextual Factors:

  • CBA goes beyond static roles and evaluates additional parameters like:
    Location: Where the user is logging in from (e.g., specific countries or regions).
    Time: The time of day or the business period during which the user is trying to access the system (e.g., access during business hours vs. after-hours).
    Device: The type of device the user is using (e.g., laptop, mobile, or tablet).
    User Attributes: Attributes tied to the user profile, such as department, job function, or security clearance level.
    Data Sensitivity: Sensitivity level of the data being accessed (e.g., financial vs. non-sensitive information).

This means that even if a user has a role that grants them access, the system can deny or modify their access depending on the context.

2. Granular Control:

  • CBA allows for fine-grained access control, meaning that specific conditions can be set for individual transactions or actions.
  • For example, a financial manager may be allowed to approve expenses up to a certain amount but only when connected to the corporate network and during business hours. If they try to approve the expense remotely or after-hours, the authorization may be restricted.

3. Improved Security:

  • Adaptive Security: By factoring in real-time contextual information, CBA improves security by adapting to potential risks. For example, the system might detect that an access request is coming from an unfamiliar location or outside normal business hours and take precautionary measures such as denying access or triggering additional authentication.
  • Risk Mitigation: It helps mitigate risks by ensuring that sensitive transactions or data can only be accessed under secure, controlled conditions.

4. Flexibility and Compliance:

  • Regulatory Compliance: Many industries are subject to strict compliance regulations that may require dynamic access controls, especially concerning sensitive data like Personally Identifiable Information (PII). CBA helps meet these requirements by ensuring that access to data is aligned with specific contextual conditions.
  • Business Flexibility: Organizations can adapt access controls to meet business requirements without hardcoding rules in the system, providing flexibility to handle exceptional situations without compromising security.

Example Use Cases:

  1. Field Service Management: A field engineer may be allowed to access customer maintenance data while they are on-site at a customer’s location, but not when they are outside the location or during non-working hours.
  2. Finance and HR: A payroll manager might have access to salary data but only within the secure office network. If the manager tries to access the same information from a public Wi-Fi network, access could be blocked or restricted to read-only.
  3. Sensitive Transactions: A sales manager can create purchase orders for high-value items but only from approved devices. If they use a mobile device, they may only be able to view sales reports but not execute high-value transactions.

Implementation in SAP:

  • Dynamic Authorization Management: CBA is often implemented using a combination of SAP Authorization Objects, ABAP coding for dynamic checks, and Security Policies that define context-based rules.
  • SAP GRC: In some cases, CBA can be integrated with SAP Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) tools to enforce risk-based authorizations, ensuring that the risk profile of the transaction and context is evaluated before access is granted.
  • Integration with Fiori and BTP: For systems using SAP Fiori or the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), context-based rules can be implemented to dynamically grant or restrict access to specific Fiori apps based on contextual information.

Benefits:

  • Enhanced Security: Limits access based on real-time conditions, reducing the attack surface.
  • Reduced Risk of Misuse: Controls over sensitive transactions and data access prevent unauthorized activities even from users with high-level roles.
  • Increased Flexibility: Business processes remain fluid without hardcoded access restrictions, as the system adjusts access based on the situation.
  • Better Compliance: Ensures that sensitive information is accessed in a controlled, compliant manner.

Context-Based Authorization in SAP provides dynamic and adaptive access control based on real-time contextual factors, enhancing security while maintaining flexibility and compliance in the organization’s access management strategy.

Wrapping up:

In conclusion, when it comes to your S/4HANA transformation journey, the strategy you choose depends on your organization’s unique needs and future landscape. Each approach offers its own set of advantages and drawbacks, so it’s not a simple, one-size-fits-all comparison.

The key is to select a strategy that aligns best with your goals. However, one critical factor to consider is maintaining a "Clean Core" This principle is likely to become a major focus in future transformations, and adhering to it now can help you avoid unnecessary rework down the road.

In short, while there are many ways to approach your S/4HANA transformation, prioritizing a Clean Core can streamline the process and future-proof your system.

entry.title: Need excel format to upload Raw Exposure using t-code FTREX_EXCEL_UPLOAD
Re: Automate the Interim Account Update rule using Integration Center Job (Time Off)
entry.content:
Hi @PetraRana Sorry for replying late! I missed your comment

1. My understanding of the proposed solution is to use MDF based business rule triggered by scheduled Integration Centre Job to post interim update instead of standard interim rule, correct? - You're correct!
2. Therefore each interim rule to be automated would be replaced by MDF based business rule on Time Account object and a corresponding Integration Job, correct? You're correct!
3. Can you please advise what is the purpose of the custom field on the time account object? and how this will get set to Yes to begin with, when the business rule sets this field to 'null' after processing? Essentially, Onsave rules for MDF objects are triggered whenever an update occurs through the UI, import, API, or an Integration Center job. My intention is for this specific rule to activate only during updates made via the Integration Center job. That's why I'm setting the default value to "Yes" in the Integration Center. Additionally, I'm resetting the field value to null to prevent the rule from executing if the data update is performed through the UI, import, API.

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements: Implement Visual Filters in Analytic List Page (ALP)
entry.content:
Thank you for this blog well explained but not working in my case.

What do you mean with "All these annotations need to be added as sub annotations of the attribute for which we are going to implement this visual filters" ?
